title: Att dwar il-Ġarr Stradali Internazzjonali ta' Merkanzija
title_en: Att dwar il-Ġarr Stradali Internazzjonali ta' Merkanzija
description: Official text from legislation.mt (eli/cap/486/mlt, expression eli/cap/486/20100101/mlt).

provisions.title: L-ATT XI tal-2006, kif emendat bl-Att XV tal-2009
provisions.content:
It-titolu fil-qosor ta’ dan l-Att huwa l-Att dwar l-Adozzjoni
tal-Euro.

provisions.title: Titolu fil-qosor
provisions.content:
F’dan l-Att, kemm-il darba r-rabta tal-kliem ma tkunx
teġtieġ xort’oħra:

Tifsir.
Emendat:
XV. 2009.49.

"l-Awtorità" tfisser l-Awtorità għat-Trasport f ’Malta kif
imwaqqfa taħt l-Att dwar l-Awtorità għat-Trasport f’Malta;

Kap. 499.

"jordna" tfisser jordna permezz ta’ ordni jew b’regolamenti skont
dan l-Att;
"Konvenzjoni CMR" tfisser il-Konvenzjoni dwar il-Kuntratt
għall-Ġarr Stradali Internazzjonali ta’ Merkanzija (CMR) iffirmata
f’Ġinevra fid-19 ta’ Mejju, 1956 kif emendata bil-Protokoll CMR
ta’ Ġinevra iffirmat fil-5 ta’ Lulju, 1978;
"il-Ministru" tfisser il-Ministru responsabbli għat-trasport
stradali.

provisions.title: Tifsir
provisions.content: Il-Gvern ta’ Malta hu b’dan awtorizzat jirratifika lKonvenzjoni CMR.

provisions.title: Ratifika talKonvenzjoni
provisions.content:
(1) Bla preġudizzju għall-applikabbiltà tal-Konvenzjoni
CMR bejn Malta u l-Pajjiżi Kontraenti l-oħrajn kollha, il-Ministru
jista’, b’ordni fil-Gazzetta, minn żmien għal żmien jispeċifika
liema jkunu l-Istati Kontraenti, minbarra Malta, għall-finijiet talKonvenzjoni CMR.

Stati Kontraenti.

(2) Ordni bħal dak għandu jkun jispeċifika d-data tad-dħul fisseħħ tal-Konvenzjoni CMR bejn Malta u Stat li jkun speċifikat flordni.

provisions.title: Stati Kontraenti
provisions.content:
(1) Id-dispożizzjonijiet tal-Konvenzjoni CMR għandu
jkollhom, minkejja dak kollu li jista’ jkun hemm kuntrarju f’kull
liġi oħra iżda bla ħsara għad-dispożizzjonijiet ta’ dan l-Att, forza
ta’ liġi ma’ Malta kollha għar-rigward tal-ġarr stradali
internazzjonali ta’ merkanzija minn dik id-data * u skont dawk ilkondizzjonijiet li l-Ministru jista’ jordna.
(2) Il-Konvenzjoni CMR tinsab fl-Iskeda u l-imsemmija skeda
tista’ tiġi emendata minn żmien għal żmien skont ma jista’ jiġi
ordnat għall-fini li tinżamm aġġornata mat-test uffiċjali talKonvenzjoni CMR.

*20 ta’ Marzu, 2008 - ara l-Avviż Legali 52 tal-2008.

Il-Konvenzjoni
CMR ikollha forza
ta’ liġi.

[KAP. 486.

ĠARR STRADALI INTERNAZZJONALI TA’ MERKANZIJA

(3) Il-Konvenzjoni CMR li tinsab fl-Iskeda qiegħda tiġi
riprodotta fl-ilsien Ingliż biss.
Poter tal-Ministru
li jagħmel
regolamenti.

provisions.title: regolamenti
provisions.content:
(1) Il-Ministru jista’ wara konsultazzjoni mal-Awtorità,
jagħmel regolamenti biex jordna kull ħaġa li tkun meħtieġa jew
spedjenti għall-iskop li jiġu ratifikati jew imwettqa l-obbligi ta’
Malta taħt il-Konvenzjoni CMR u b’mod ġenerali għat-twettiq taddispożizzjonijiet ta’ dan l-Att u, bla preġudizzju għall-ġeneralità
ta’ dak hawn qabel imsemmi, jista’ jagħmel regolamenti:
(a) dwar il-ġarr stradali ta’ merkanzija, salvi ddispożizzjonijiet ta’ kull liġi li tirregola t-trasport jew
il-ġarr stradali ta’ merkanzija; u
(b) biex jemenda l-Iskeda kollha kemm hi jew f’parti
minnha.
(2) Il-Ministru jista’ wkoll jipprovdi li kull regolament jew
ordni taħt dan l-Att inklużi r-riservi li jkunu magħmulin minn
Malta għall-Konvenzjoni CRM jew kull dikjarazzjoni li tkun
m a g ħ m u l a m i n n M a l t a w a q t l - i ff i r m a r j e w i r- r a t i f i k a t a l Konvenzjoni CMR, għandhom jidħlu bħala skedi ma’ dan l-Att.

ĠARR STRADALI INTERNAZZJONALI TA’ MERKANZIJA

[KAP. 486.

(Artikolu 5)
Flok il-kliem "Contracting Party" għandhom jidħlu jew il-kelma "Malta" jew ilkliem "Contracting State" skont il-każ.
OF GOODS BY ROAD (CMR) DONE AT GENEVA ON THE 19TH OF MAY, 1956,
ON THE 5TH OF JULY, 1978
Chapter 1- Scope of Application
Chapter II- Persons for whom the Carrier is Responsible
Chapter III- Conclusion and Performance of the Contract of Carriage
Chapter IV- Liability of the Carrier
Chapter V- Claims and Actions
Chapter VI- Provisions Relating to Carriage Performed by Successive Carriers
Chapter VII- Nullity of Stipulation to the Convention
Chapter VIII- Final Provisions
The Contracting Parties,
Having recognized the desirability of standardizing the conditions governing the
contract for the international carriage of goods by road, particularly with respect to
the documents used for such carriage and to the carrier’s liability,
Have agreed as follows:
Chapter 1 - Scope of Application
Article 1

provisions.title: notify the person who gave him such instructions
provisions.content:
A carrier who has not carried out the instructions given under the conditions
provided for in this article or who has carried them out without requiring the first
copy of the consignment note to be produced, shall be liable to the person entitled to
make a claim for any loss or damage caused thereby.
Article 13

provisions.title: State
provisions.content:
Nevertheless, a State which is not a member of the International Monetary
Fund and whose law does not permit the application of the provisions of paragraph 7
of this article may, at the time of ratification of or accession to the Protocol to the
CMR or at any time thereafter, declare that the limit of liability provided for in
paragraph 3 of this article to be applied in its territory shall be 25 monetary units.
The monetary unit referred to in this paragraph corresponds to the 10/31 gram of
gold of millesimal fineness nine hundred. The conversion shall be made according to
the law of the State concerned.

provisions.title: State
provisions.content:
The calculation mentioned in the last sentence of paragraph 7 of this article
and the conversion mentioned in paragraph 8 of this article shall be made in such a
manner as to express in the national currency of the State as far as possible the same
real value for the amount in paragraph 3 of this article as is expressed there in units
of account. States shall communicate to the Secretary-General of the United Nations

[KAP. 486.

ĠARR STRADALI INTERNAZZJONALI TA’ MERKANZIJA

the manner of calculation pursuant to paragraph 7 of this article or the result of the
conversion in paragraph 8 of this article as the case may be, when depositing an
instrument referred to in Article 3 of the Protocol to the CMR and whenever there is
a change in either.
Article 24
The sender may, against payment of a surcharge to be agreed upon, declare in the
consignment note a value for the goods exceeding the limit laid down in article 23,
paragraph 3, and in that case the amount of the declared value shall be substituted for
that limit.
Article 25
